description: Primary-sourced motion/animation rules — duration + easing guidance, performant properties, and the reduced-motion contract. Re-derived from Material Design motion, MDN, and WCAG.

rules.title: UI transition durations 150–500ms
rules.rule: Most UI transitions land 150–300ms; larger/complex surfaces up to ~500ms. Too fast feels abrupt, too slow feels sluggish. Small elements move faster than large ones.

rules.title: Use asymmetric easing, not linear
rules.rule: Natural motion uses ease-out for entering (fast→slow, decelerate) and ease-in for exiting; linear() reads mechanical. Standard curve ~cubic-bezier(0.2, 0, 0, 1).

rules.title: Animate only transform + opacity
rules.rule: Animate compositor-friendly properties (transform, opacity) to stay at 60fps; animating layout properties (width, top, margin) triggers layout/paint and jank. Promote with will-change sparingly.
rules.dont: transition: width / left (forces reflow each frame).

rules.title: Honor prefers-reduced-motion
rules.rule: Under @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce), remove or strongly reduce non-essential motion (parallax, large transitions, autoplay); vestibular disorders are triggered by motion. Cross-references WCAG 2.3.3.

rules.title: Motion is functional, not decorative
rules.rule: Animation should direct attention, show relationships/continuity (where a thing came from), and give feedback — not decorate. Gratuitous motion adds cognitive load.
